question.text: A call option buyer would exercise the option only if:

answer.text: The price of the underlying asset is higher than the strike price at expiry
The price of the underlying asset is lower than the strike price at expiry
The option premium has increased since purchase

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A call buyer exercises when spot > strike because they can buy at the (lower) strike and profit. If spot ≤ strike, the call expires worthless — no rational buyer exercises an OTM call. **The Trap:** Premium increase alone doesn't require exercise — the buyer could simply sell the option for a profit instead. **Cross-Exam Context:** Exercise decision is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Similarly, a put buyer exercises only when spot < strike (can sell at the higher strike price).
